channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17893] replay-dual test_31: 7 multiops failed
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/8e1bdc6e-77a4-469e-a4de-5b505113138e

test_31 failed with the following error:

file /mnt/lustre2/d31.replay-dual/mdtdir/f31.replay-dual is ready
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
open(O_RDWR|O_CREAT): No space left on device
:
pids: 121763 121765 121770 121771 121772 121773 121774 121775 121776
wait 121770 failed, rc = 28
wait 121771 failed, rc = 28
wait 121772 failed, rc = 28
wait 121773 failed, rc = 28
wait 121774 failed, rc = 28
wait 121775 failed, rc = 28
wait 121776 failed, rc = 28
 replay-dual test_31: @@@@@@ FAIL: 7 multiops failed

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/105098 - 5.14.0-362.18.1.el9_3.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/105098 - 5.14.0-362.18.1_lustre.el9.x86_64

It looks like this has failed on master in the past 6 months only since 2024-05-29 when the most recent batch of patches landed, and on master-next since 2024-05-23 with the same patches, so it is likely related to a new patch landing. However, there are no patches that changed replay-dual directly, and the only other test that is running before replay-dual in this session is mds-survey which was also not modified.

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
replay-dual test_31 - 7 multiops failed

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56712/
Subject: LU-17893 tests: wait destroys before replay-dual:test_28
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 14ddf92d8f8fed7994993072bdfbe55917151820

"Vladimir Saveliev <vladimir.saveliev@hpe.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56712
Subject: LU-17893 tests: wait destroys before replay-dual:test_28
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 23b21a8e3428521ccbebf8ac250b56c896715803

replay-dual.sh:test_28() switches "drop blocking asts" mode on for 2 seconds:

..
        dd if=/dev/zero of=$DIR2/$tfile bs=4096 count=1

	#define OBD_FAIL_LDLM_SRV_BL_AST         0x324
        do_facet ost1 $LCTL set_param fail_loc=0x80000324

        dd if=/dev/zero of=$DIR/$tfile bs=4096 count=1 &
	local pid=$!
        sleep 2

	#define OBD_FAIL_LDLM_GRANT_CHECK        0x32a
        do_facet ost1 $LCTL set_param fail_loc=0x32a

It is supposed to be only one dropped blocking ast for enqueue coming from cl_setattr_ost when second dd does truncate.
However, previous test_26() leaves number of pending destroys. It seems that dropping of all blocking asts during 2 seconds causes things like:

[169376.453554] Lustre: ll_ost00_057: service thread pid 236757 was inactive for 40.816 seconds. Watchdog stack traces are limited to 3 per 300 seconds, skipping this one.
[169376.461659] [<0>] ldlm_completion_ast+0x99b/0xc00 [ptlrpc]
[169376.461782] [<0>] ldlm_cli_enqueue_local+0x302/0x890 [ptlrpc]
[169376.461888] [<0>] ofd_destroy_by_fid+0x29c/0x570 [ofd]
[169376.461906] [<0>] ofd_destroy_hdl+0x22c/0x960 [ofd]
[169376.461919] [<0>] tgt_request_handle+0x3f4/0x1a30 [ptlrpc]

and

[169638.155933] LustreError: 236757:0:(ldlm_request.c:104:ldlm_expired_completion_wait()) ### lock timed out (enqueued at 1729087746, 303s ago); not entering recovery in server code, just going back to sleep ns: filter-lustre-OST0005_UUID lock: ffff96e3e8d673c0/0x8436b743528d7e5c lrc: 3/0,1 mode: --/PW res: [0x440000400:0x476:0x0].0x0 rrc: 3 type: EXT [0->18446744073709551615] (req 0->18446744073709551615) gid 0 flags: 0x40010080000000 nid: local remote: 0x0 expref: -99 pid: 236757 timeout: 0 lvb_type: 0

and

[169852.021044] Lustre: ll_ost00_052: service thread pid 236555 completed after 516.964s. This likely indicates the system was overloaded (too many service threads, or not enough hardware resources).

Note, that using CFS_FAIL_ONCE (fail_loc=0x80000324) does not make affect in:

        if (CFS_FAIL_PRECHECK(OBD_FAIL_LDLM_SRV_BL_AST)) {
                LDLM_DEBUG(lock, "dropping BL AST");
                RETURN(0);
        }

so, many blocking asts get dropped in those 2 seconds.

I am not sure, that this causes the test_31() problem (I was not able to reproduce it yet), but I assume the described problem worth fixing.

Lustre 2.16.0 RC2 test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4584 - 4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_10.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4584 - 4.18.0-553.16.1.el8_lustre.x86_64
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/b30f1f98-49e6-4d3c-9cdd-99091acf6598

wait 1269144 failed, rc = 28
...
600	/mnt/lustre/drr_alloc.parallel-scale
84	/mnt/lustre/f30.replay-dual
56	/mnt/lustre/d31.replay-dual
20	/mnt/lustre/d29.replay-dual
4	/mnt/lustre/f28.replay-dual
4	/mnt/lustre/f25.replay-dual
4	/mnt/lustre/d26.replay-dual
4	/mnt/lustre/d18.replay-dual
0	/mnt/lustre/f17.replay-dual-2-0
du: cannot access '/mnt/lustre/drr_alloc.parallel-scale/*': No such file or directory
 replay-dual test_31: @@@@@@ FAIL: 7 multiops failed due to '/mnt/lustre/drr_alloc.parallel-scale'

This failure occurred 10 times in the last 6 months.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55321/
Subject: LU-17893 tests: replay-dual/31 fails a lot
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c6b9f30f7eb8590acd8ee5088cfa2b9f8102c5f6

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55321
Subject: LU-17893 tests: replay-dual/31 fails a lot
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: b38f91f20b3012d6ef7c1a72e3b31cfe3877bd9f
It probably makes sense to add some debugging to the test to run "lfs df" to confirm out-of-space and find directories that are using a lot of space.
